summary: Stellar Estimated→Eyes transition and soaring Stella Blue
blurb: The second set dominates with a benchmark Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World segue, a hypnotic Stella Blue, and tight rhythm work throughout. Jerry’s guitar is incandescent, Phil’s bass beefy, and Keith provides razor‑sharp keys.
review: May 18 1977 at Atlanta’s Fox Theater captures the Grateful Dead in peak spring‑’77 form. The first set rolls solidly through classics like Jack‑A‑Roe and a bright Lazy Lightning → Supplication pair, but it’s the second set that truly shines. A seamless Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World transition launches into an expansive jam that rides Jerry’s “mission” guitar tone and Phil’s booming low end. The band then drifts into a long, hypnotic Stella Blue before closing with The Other One and a rousing Around & Around. Keith’s keyboards are sharp yet unobtrusive, and both Mickey and Bill lock in perfectly with the guitars, delivering a rhythmic foundation that feels both relaxed and propulsive. With crystal‑clear soundboard fidelity, this show stands out as one of the era’s benchmark performances.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Estimated Prophet → Eyes sequence, the soaring Stella Blue, and Jerry’s incendiary guitar work. Multiple sources highlight tight rhythm sections, strong keyboard textures, and flawless soundboard quality, indicating strong consensus of excellence.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with superb fidelity, clear instrument separation, and the most complete representation of the standout Estimated→Eyes and Stella Blue moments.

key_highlights: Benchmark Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World segue
Long, hypnotic Stella Blue jam in the second set
Samba‑style Jack‑A‑Roe that drives the groove
Lazy Lightning → Supplication transition adds energetic sparkle

band_performance.Jerry: Incendiary guitar tone with soaring solos, especially on Estimated Prophet and Eyes of the World; described as ‘mission’ vibe and flawless phrasing.
band_performance.Phil: Beautiful, beefy bass that provides a solid foundation; praised for booming low‑end power despite occasional mix dip early in Deal.
band_performance.Bob: Tight rhythm work, driving Jack‑A‑Roe and delivering strong vocal presence on Ship Of Fools and Stella Blue.
band_performance.Brent: Sharp, unplugged yet razor‑sharp keyboard playing by Keith; adds eerie piano texture to Friend of the Devil and supports the jam sections beautifully.
band_performance.Mickey: Contributions noted positively, adding depth and complementary percussion throughout the set.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming intertwines perfectly with Jerry’s guitar, providing a perfect rhythmic match.
